data.title: ACM Research expects to report Q1 revenue growth of 31% to 33%
data.description:
• ACM Research (ACMR) said on Monday that its preliminary unaudited revenue for the first quarter of 2026 is expected to be in the range of $225M to $230M (vs. estimates of $216.47M), which would represent year-to-year growth of 31% to 33%.
  • Preliminary total shipments for the first quarter of 2026 are expected to be in the range of $233M to $238M, translating to a year-to-year growth of 49% to 52%.  
  • It also reaffirmed thefull-year 2026 revenue outlook in the range of $1.08B to $1.175B, representing year-to-year growth of 20% to 30%.
  • ACM will discuss its full financial results on Thursday, May 7, 2026, before markets open.
  • ACMR -0.25% premarket to $55.96.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ralph Lauren leads all large-cap consumer discretionary stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap consumer discretionary stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors, including Apparel, Accessories and Luxury Goods, Restaurants, Home Furnishings, Automotive Retail, Hotels, Resorts and Cruise Lines, Broadline Retail, Casinos and Gaming, and Automobile Manufacturers.

Ralph Lauren (RL) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 147.79% year-over-year, driven by continued investment in retail store expansion and brand elevation, with total capital expenditures rising to $216 million in Fiscal 2025 from $165 million in the prior year.

DoorDash (DASH) and Somnigroup International (SGI) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 147.12% and 71.53% respectively, while Carvana (CVNA) and Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) round out the top five at 61.54% and 60.01%.

Amazon (AMZN) and Wynn Resorts (WYNN) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 58.82% and 57.27%, while DraftKings (DKNG) and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) follow at 50.86% and 49.87%.

CAVA Group (CAVA) rounds out the list at 46.77%, reflecting the Mediterranean fast-casual chain's ongoing restaurant expansion, with 72 net new locations opened in fiscal 2025.

Somnigroup International (SGI) is the only stock on the list with a Sell rating at 2.02, while the remaining names all carry Hold ratings ranging from 2.64 to 3.44 under Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings, making this one of the few cross-sector CapEx growth lists without a single Buy or Strong Buy recommendation.

Here is the list:

  1. Ralph Lauren Corporation (RL), CapEx growth YoY: 147.79%

  2. DoorDash, Inc. (DASH), CapEx growth YoY: 147.12%

  3. Somnigroup International Inc. (SGI), CapEx growth YoY: 71.53%

  4. Carvana Co. (CVNA), CapEx growth YoY: 61.54%

  5. Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (RCL), CapEx growth YoY: 60.01%

  6. Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), CapEx growth YoY: 58.82%

  7. Wynn Resorts, Limited (WYNN), CapEx growth YoY: 57.27%

  8. DraftKings Inc. (DKNG), CapEx growth YoY: 50.86%

  9. Rivian Automotive, Inc. (RIVN), CapEx growth YoY: 49.87%

  10. CAVA Group, Inc. (CAVA), CapEx growth YoY: 46.77%

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: GameSquare reports record Q1 bookings for experiential marketing unit
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) on Monday said its GameSquare Experiences 'GSX' division generated over $10 million in bookings in the first quarter of 2026, marking a record performance.
  • The company said growth was driven by demand from video game publishers and global brands, alongside increased adoption of its digital-to-physical marketing campaigns.
  • GameSquare said GSX has expanded its recurring client base more than threefold since its launch in 2024 and increased revenue about 14 times over the same period.
  • The division focuses on experiential and creator-led marketing campaigns that integrate digital content with real-world activations.
  • GAME +6.86% premarket to $0.638.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Adobe downgraded at Mizuho amid 'weakened' conviction
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday after Mizuho Securities downgraded the Photoshop maker, citing “weakened” conviction.

“Our conviction has weakened as we see intensifying competition in the prosumer/SMB segments threatening ADBE's long-term terminal value, along with no clear catalyst for the stock, and a risk of margin erosion,” analyst Gregg Moskowitz wrote in a note to clients. 

Moskowitz continued: “While we believe ADBE is making some more progress monetizing its Generative AI innovations, we also believe its organic revenue/ARR CAGR over the next 2-3 years is high-single-digits at best, and we much prefer owning assets at very reasonable (if not depressed) valuations that possess meaningful potential for upward estimate revisions. We see a generally balanced risk/reward profile on ADBE from here, and are thus downgrading to Neutral from Outperform and lowering our PT to $270 from $315.”

Delving deeper, Moskowitz said that he has become "incrementally more concerned"as Adobe's consumer and small-and-medium-sized business tiers face greater competition from AI-native platforms and low-cost design tools. And with the barriers to entry falling, lower-end users may bypass Adobe's Creative Cloud altogether, Moskowitz posited. 

Additionally, Moskowitz said he does not see a catalyst that can re-rate the stock over the next 6 to 12 months, as revenue guidance implies only 9.5% year-over-year growth, below management's aspiration for double-digit growth. There are only concerns that margins could decline, due to the aforementioned competitive threats and rising inference costs for Firefly.

data.title: Drug developer Seaport Therapeutics seeks up to $212M in US IPO
data.description:
Seaport Therapeutics (SPTX) on Monday filed for an initial public offering of 11.8M shares priced between $16 and $18 each, seeking to raise as much as about $212M.  

At $18 per share, the clinical-stage biotech firm will have a market value of $912M.

The company has applied to list its shares on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker SPTX. 

Goldman Sachs (GS), JP Morgan (JPM), and Leerink Partners are leading the offering.

Seaport, founded in 2024, is developing oral therapies for neuropsychiatric diseases such as depression using its proprietary Glyph platform. Its lead candidate, GlyphAllo, is being studied as a treatment for major depressive disorder, with mid-stage trial results expected in the first half of 2027.

Earlier this month, the company reported positive early-stage data for a second candidate targeting generalized anxiety disorder and said it plans to advance the treatment into two parallel studies.

Seaport reported a net loss of $74.9M last year, compared with a loss of $46.9M a year earlier, according to a previous filing.

PureTech Health plc owns nearly 50% of Seaport following funding rounds that have raised $325M since inception. Other investors include affiliates of Arch Venture Partners and General Atlantic.

Chief Executive Officer Daphne Zohar previously led Karuna Therapeutics until its $14B sale to Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) in 2024.

data.title: Tech's new record comes without peak-valuation heat
data.description:
Technology stocks (XLK) have recently climbed back to record territory, but the rally looks fundamentally different from last year’s peak.

According to Duality Research, the sector’s forward price-to-earnings ratio now sits at 23.3 times, roughly eight multiple points below its October 2025 high. 

While tech valuations remain above the 10-year average of 22.8 times, they have fallen below the five-year average of 25.7 times.

The shift suggests that rising earnings expectations have driven more of the gains rather than expanding valuations alone. In practical terms, the market is still paying a premium for tech companies, though not the stretched premium investors were willing to accept at the height of the 2025 artificial intelligence (AIQ) trade.

data.title: It's the 'most important week' for stock returns (watch chips) - SocGen
data.description:
This is the "most important" week for U.S. equity returns, with a heavy concentration of earnings and policy signals set to drive markets, according to Société Générale.

Strategist Manish Kabra said roughly $29T in market cap, or about 44% of the S&P 500 (SPY) (IVV) (VOO), is reporting results, making it the most critical stretch of the earnings season. SocGen expects 2026 EPS growth to remain positive across all sectors, led by Technology (XLK) — particularly semiconductors (SMH) (SOXX) — along with Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB).

The firm highlighted the scale of investment underpinning the outlook, noting that major techn companies reporting this week have committed to about $676B in AI-related capex for 2026.

Semiconductors are seen as the dominant force behind market performance. The SOX index is up 42% YTD, compared with a 2% gain for the Magnificent 7 (AAPL) (AMZN) (GOOG) (GOOGL) (META) (MSFT) (NVDA) (TSLA) and just 0.2% excluding Nvidia, underscoring the sector’s outsized influence. Apple, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta and Microsoft all report this week.

SocGen estimates semiconductors have added about $2.2T in market cap, accounting for 84% of total gains, and are responsible for roughly 75% of U.S. tech EPS growth.

Excluding semiconductors, the growth profile weakens significantly, with tech EPS growth falling back toward market levels and S&P 500 earnings growth easing to around 12%.

data.title: Google likely to work with MediaTek for 8th-gen TPUs: report
data.description:
• Following Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) announcement of its eighth-generation tensor processor units, Taiwan news outlet Commercial Times says the U.S. tech giant is likely to work with MediaTek for part of the upcoming chips. 
  • MediaTek is likely to work on providing the I/O dies and back-end design services, also known as packaging, according to the news outlet, which cited its sources. Google will do the computing core design for the TPU 8t. Broadcom (AVGO) is set to handle the TPU 8i, the news outlet added. In addition, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) is set to handle the manufacturing of the TPU 8t on its 3-nanometer technology and provide its chip-on-a-wafer substrate advanced packaging technology. 
  • Google, MediaTek, Taiwan Semiconductor, and Broadcom did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • The TPU 8t was designed for training artificial intelligence models, while the TPU 8i was built for running inference. The two chips were announced at the Google Cloud Next 2026 event in Las Vegas earlier this month.

data.title: Townsquare, NABCO form digital advertising partnership
data.description:
• Townsquare Media (TSQ) on Monday said it has entered a strategic digital advertising partnership with North American Broadcasting Company (NABCO).
  • Under the agreement, Townsquare will provide its digital advertising technology, platform, and data-driven services to support NABCO’s digital growth.
  • The partnership is part of Townsquare’s Media Partnerships division, launched in 2024, which offers white-label digital advertising solutions to local media companies.
  • Townsquare said the NABCO alliance is one of 11 such partnerships, expanding its reach into 31 additional markets.
  • TSQ +3.45% premarket to $6.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oxford Lane Capital to redeem 6.25% Series 2027 Term Preferred Stock
data.description: Oxford Lane Capital (OXLC) said it will redeem all outstanding shares of its 6.25% Series 2027 Term Preferred Stock (OXLCP) on June 1, 2026, for $25 per share, or about $18.1M in total. The shares will be delisted from the Nasdaq Global Select Market after the redemption.
